message:
fix(http): correctly handle HEAD responses by reusing streaming impl

Refactor Http1Client::request() to delegate to request_streaming()
instead of using the Framed codec directly. This ensures HEAD responses
correctly ignore Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding body indicators
per RFC 7230 §3.3, since the streaming implementation already has
method-aware body parsing logic.

The new implementation:
- Calls request_streaming() to get the parsed response head and a
  streaming body reader that respects the HTTP method semantics
- Collects body frames via poll_frame() into a Vec<u8>, properly
  handling Buf::chunk()/advance() for correct BytesMut consumption
- Collects trailer frames into the response trailers vector
- Adds an explicit io.flush().await after write_all() in
  request_streaming() to ensure request bytes are fully sent before
  waiting for the response (prevents hangs on buffered transports)

Removes now-unused imports: Framed codec wrapper, Stream trait.

Adds test `request_head_response_ignores_content_length_body` which
sends a HEAD request, receives a response with Content-Length: 5 but
no body bytes, and verifies the response body is empty (previously
this would hang waiting for 5 bytes that the server never sends).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
